Vertex Labs

Mid Product Designer

LOCATION

San Francisco, CA
(Hybrid: 3 days/week
in office)

TYPE

Full-time

COMPENSATION

$120,000 - $155,000
per year

TECH STACK

Figma (design,
prototyping, design
systems)

Interaction Design

Design Systems &
Component Libraries

User Research &
Testing

Prototyping &
Animation

Cross-functional
Collaboration Tools

Wireframing &
Information
Architecture

Usability Testing &
Analytics

About Vertex Labs: Vertex Labs is a growing
design-focused SaaS company building tools that
help teams collaborate and communicate better.
We believe great design transcends aesthetics—it
solves real problems and scales with intention.
Our platform serves over 50,000 teams across
finance, healthcare, and creative industries,
and we're on a mission to make design and
product collaboration accessible to everyone.

We're seeking a Mid Product Designer to join our
growing Design team and take ownership of key
feature areas within our core product. In this
role, you'll work closely with product managers,
engineers, and stakeholders to design intuitive,
delightful experiences. You'll conduct user

research, create prototypes, build design
systems components, and iterate based on
feedback. This is a great opportunity to grow as
a designer, develop strategic thinking, and
directly impact the product roadmap.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Own end-to-end design for assigned feature
areas, from discovery and wireframing through
high-fidelity prototypes in Figma

Conduct user interviews and usability testing
to validate design decisions and identify pain

points in existing workflows

Create and maintain components within our
design system using Figma, ensuring
consistency across the platform

Design interactive prototypes and user flows
using Figma prototyping and animation features
to communicate interactions with engineering

Collaborate with cross-functional teams
(product, engineering, marketing) to define
requirements, scope, and success metrics for
new features

Participate in design critiques and provide
constructive feedback to junior designers and

Designing intuitiveexperiences that connectpeople to what matters most teammates

Document design specifications and handoff
deliverables to ensure engineering accuracy
during implementation

Iterate on designs based on A/B testing
results, user feedback, and analytics data

Contribute to design system improvements and
advocate for design consistency across
products

REQUIREMENTS

2-5 years of professional product design
experience in SaaS, web, or mobile
applications

Proficiency with Figma and ability to design
high-fidelity mockups and interactive
prototypes

Strong foundational knowledge of interaction
design principles and user-centered design
methodology

Portfolio demonstrating design process,
problem-solving, and attention to detail
across multiple projects

Experience collaborating with cross-functional
teams and communicating design rationale to
non-designers

Basic understanding of design systems and
component-based design thinking

BONUS POINTS

Experience with design system ownership and
component library management

Familiarity with user research methods and
ability to synthesize findings into actionable
insights

Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG
2.1) and inclusive design practices

Experience with prototyping tools beyond Figma
(Framer, Principle, or similar)

BENEFITS

 Competitive health, dental, and vision
insurance (company covers 85% of employee
premiums)

401(k) with 4% company match and immediate
vesting

Unlimited PTO (with a minimum of 15 days
encouraged)

Professional development budget of $2,000/year
for courses, conferences, and design tools

Flexible work schedule and hybrid work
arrangement

Bi-weekly catered lunches and fully stocked
kitchen

APPLY

Submit your resume, cover letter, and portfolio
link (Figma case studies preferred) to
careers@vertexlabs.com with the subject line
'Mid Product Designer Application'. We review
applications on a rolling basis. Interview
process includes a portfolio review, take-home
design exercise, and multiple rounds of
interviews with the design and product
leadership team.

design exercise, and multiple rounds of
interviews with the design and product
